data.intent_description: Resolve routing loop/blackhole: 192.168.0.12 cannot reach 172.16.0.12 because R1 and R3 have mutual static routes for 172.16.0.0/24 pointing at each other; after remediation, traffic from 192.168.0.12 to 172.16.0.12 should follow a correct path (no mutual static next-hops).

plan.problem: Mutual static routes on router1 and router3 for 172.16.0.0/24 (each pointing at the other) create a forwarding loop/blackhole, preventing reachability to 172.16.0.12.
plan.fix_summary: Remove the incorrect static route on router3 that points 172.16.0.0/24 at router1 (next-hop 10.0.0.10). Risk: traffic to 172.16.0.0/24 may be temporarily unreachable until a correct route to the network (via router2) is installed or router2 is confirmed reachable. Assumption: router2 is the correct path to 172.16.0.0/24 and will be handled separately if needed.

evidence: R3 running-config (routing.ip-route-interface-forwarding-list) contains only a default route (prefix 0.0.0.0 via Loopback1); there is no static route for 172.16.0.0/24 in R3's config (tool output from get_running_config for R3).
R1 running-config still shows a static route entry for 172.16.0.0/24 with next-hop 10.0.0.9 (tool output from get_running_config for R1).
Ping executed from R1 to 172.16.0.12 returned: '!!!!!\r\nSuccess rate is 100 percent (5/5), round-trip min/avg/max = 5/5/5 ms' (tool output from ping).
remaining_issues: router2 management/config fetch failed: 'All connection attempts failed' — router2 operational/config state remains unknown and should be validated.
R1 still has a static route for 172.16.0.0/24 via 10.0.0.9; confirm this route is intentional (or remove/update it) now that R3's incorrect reciprocal static route has been removed.
Reachability from the original host 192.168.0.12 to 172.16.0.12 was not directly tested by the verification steps; consider testing the actual client-to-client flow if required.

R1 interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0 ip 192.168.0.1/24 description 'LAN'
R1 interface Serial0/1/0 ip 10.0.0.1/30 description 'Link to R2'
R1 interface Serial0/1/1 ip 10.0.0.10/30 description 'Link to R3'
R3 interface Serial0/1/0 ip 10.0.0.9/30 description 'Link to R1'
R3 interface Serial0/1/1 ip 10.0.0.6/30 description 'Link to R2'
R3 Loopback1 ip 203.0.113.1/32 description 'Simulated Internet'
R1 has outbound access-group ACL R1_TO_R2 applied on interface Serial0/1/0
R3 has inbound access-group ACL BLOCK_FTP applied on Loopback1
R1 static/route entry: route 172.16.0.0/24 via next-hop 10.0.0.9 (from ip-route-interface-forwarding-list fwd-list)

meta.v: original routing block truncated in tool output; extracted next-hop 10.0.0.9 for prefix 172.16.0.0/24

root_causes.cause: R1 and R3 both have static route entries for 172.16.0.0/24 pointing at each other, creating a forwarding loop/blackhole for traffic from 192.168.0.12 to 172.16.0.12.
evidence: R1 routing entry: ip-route-interface-forwarding-list: { "prefix": "172.16.0.0", "mask": "255.255.255.0", "fwd-list": [{ "fwd": "10.0.0.9" }] }
R1 interface: Serial0/1/1 address 10.0.0.10/30 (link to R3)
R3 routing entry: ip-route-interface-forwarding-list: { "prefix": "172.16.0.0", "mask": "255.255.255.0", "fwd-list": [{ "fwd": "10.0.0.10" }] }
R3 interface: Serial0/1/0 address 10.0.0.9/30 (link to R1)

root_causes.cause: Router2's configuration and operational state could not be obtained (management connection failed) and its interfaces were not discovered; if 172.16.0.12 is located via R2 this device being down/unreachable would contribute to the failure.
evidence: get_running_config:router2 returned error: "All connection attempts failed"
Topology evidence: R1 Serial0/1/0 description 'Link to R2' (peer not discovered in this run) and R3 Serial0/1/1 description 'Link to R2' (peer not discovered in this run)

plan.problem: Mutual static routes on router1 and router3 for 172.16.0.0/24 (each pointing at the other) create a forwarding loop/blackhole, preventing reachability to 172.16.0.12.
plan.fix_summary: Remove the incorrect static route on router3 that points 172.16.0.0/24 at router1 (next-hop 10.0.0.10). Risk: traffic to 172.16.0.0/24 may be temporarily unreachable until a correct route to the network (via router2) is installed or router2 is confirmed reachable. Assumption: router2 is the correct path to 172.16.0.0/24 and will be handled separately if needed.

evidence: R3 running-config (routing.ip-route-interface-forwarding-list) contains only a default route (prefix 0.0.0.0 via Loopback1); there is no static route for 172.16.0.0/24 in R3's config (tool output from get_running_config for R3).
R1 running-config still shows a static route entry for 172.16.0.0/24 with next-hop 10.0.0.9 (tool output from get_running_config for R1).
Ping executed from R1 to 172.16.0.12 returned: '!!!!!\r\nSuccess rate is 100 percent (5/5), round-trip min/avg/max = 5/5/5 ms' (tool output from ping).
remaining_issues: router2 management/config fetch failed: 'All connection attempts failed' — router2 operational/config state remains unknown and should be validated.
R1 still has a static route for 172.16.0.0/24 via 10.0.0.9; confirm this route is intentional (or remove/update it) now that R3's incorrect reciprocal static route has been removed.
Reachability from the original host 192.168.0.12 to 172.16.0.12 was not directly tested by the verification steps; consider testing the actual client-to-client flow if required.

messages.content:
What was observed
- R1 and R3 both had static routes for 172.16.0.0/24 pointing at each other (R1 -> 10.0.0.9, R3 -> 10.0.0.10), creating a forwarding loop/blackhole for traffic bound to 172.16.0.0/24.

Diagnosis (root cause)
- Mutual static-route misconfiguration on R1 and R3: each router used the other as next-hop for 172.16.0.0/24.

Changes made
- Removed the incorrect static route for 172.16.0.0/24 from router3 (the entry pointing to 10.0.0.10 was deleted).

Verification — passed/failed and evidence
- Passed (partial verification):
  - R3 running-config no longer contains a static route for 172.16.0.0/24.
  - R1 still has the static route for 172.16.0.0/24 via 10.0.0.9 (intact).
  - Ping from R1 to 172.16.0.12 succeeded: 5/5 replies, 100% success (min/avg/max 5/5/5 ms).

Remaining items / risks
- router2 management/config could not be fetched ("All connection attempts failed"); router2 state and the intended path to 172.16.0.0/24 remain unverified.
- R1 still has a static route for 172.16.0.0/24 via 10.0.0.9 — confirm whether this is still required or should be updated to point toward router2.
- End-to-end test from the original host (192.168.0.12) to 172.16.0.12 was not performed; recommend that client-to-client reachability be verified once router2 is validated.

If you want, I can: (a) try to fetch router2 config/management status, (b) remove/update the static on R1 as you prefer, or (c) run an end-to-end test from the LAN host.

What was observed
- R1 and R3 both had static routes for 172.16.0.0/24 pointing at each other (R1 -> 10.0.0.9, R3 -> 10.0.0.10), creating a forwarding loop/blackhole for traffic bound to 172.16.0.0/24.

Diagnosis (root cause)

- Removed the incorrect static route for 172.16.0.0/24 from router3 (the entry pointing to 10.0.0.10 was deleted).

Verification — passed/failed and evidence

  - R3 running-config no longer contains a static route for 172.16.0.0/24.
  - R1 still has the static route for 172.16.0.0/24 via 10.0.0.9 (intact).

If you want, I can: (a) try to fetch router2 config/management status, (b) remove/update the static on R1 as you prefer, or (c) run an end-to-end test from the LAN host.
